note: CTO activated for solo-size project 'simple-todo-list-app'.
Full-stack developer activated as sole implementer (solo size).
Kickoff complete. Phase advanced from null to discovery. brief.md and tasks.json initialized.
Goal: a minimal todo list app supporting add, list/view, complete, and remove items. Single user, no auth.
Scope: keep the product to ONE tiny self-contained file; the aim is a working minimal app and a clean process loop, not a feature-rich product.
Tech approach: single self-contained HTML file in the project (no build step, no dependencies) so a solo generalist can ship it in one turn and a user can open it directly in a browser.
Risks: scope creep (mitigate by one-task roadmap); no dedicated QA/DevOps in solo (mitigate: CTO covers review and integration per PROTOCOL §7).
Discovery complete. Phase advanced from discovery to planning.
Plan: single milestone M1 with one implementation task (T1) given the one-file scope; then CTO-covered review and integration.
Wrote state/roadmap.md: goal, M1 milestone, sequence, and solo coverage notes (CTO covers QA and integration).
Planning complete. Phase advanced from planning to breakdown.
Created task T1 'Todo list app (single file)' and wrote specs/T1.md with goal, scope, files, and acceptance criteria. (CTO covering software-architect in solo, §7.)
Assigned T1 to fullstack-developer; status pending -> assigned. (CTO covering engineering-manager in solo, §7.)
Breakdown complete. Phase advanced from breakdown to implementation.
Picked up assigned task T1. Read specs/T1.md. Status assigned -> in_progress.
Wrote todo.html: one self-contained file, no build/deps, implementing add/list/complete/remove. Recorded in T1 artifacts[].
T1 implementation complete; status in_progress -> in_review. Handing off to CTO (covering QA in solo, §7) for acceptance-criteria review.
All implementation tasks are in_review. Phase advanced from implementation to review.

CTO covering QA (§7): verified all 4 acceptance criteria of T1 against todo.html (exactly one self-contained file, no build/deps, names and implements all four operations, artifact recorded). PASS. Status in_review -> done.
All roadmap tasks are done. Phase advanced from review to integration.
CTO covering DevOps (§7): integration check — todo.html is present, well-formed (matching html tags), self-contained with zero external dependencies, opens in a browser, no build step needed.
Integration passed and all roadmap tasks done. Phase advanced from integration to done.
Project 'simple-todo-list-app' complete: 1/1 task done, integration passed. Product is todo.html.

Disclosure (§5): seq 21 reused event type 'artifact_written' for the integration readiness check, but no new artifact was produced and T1.artifacts[] was not extended. PROTOCOL §5's event vocabulary has no integration/verification/readiness type; 'artifact_written' was the closest available. The integration check itself was real and passed (see seq 21 note); only the chosen event label is approximate.
